key_observations: Clear V-shaped recovery pattern: 8-week decline from 16.9km to 6.3km (May-June), then sharp resurgence to 15.7km in week of 06-22 with 3 runs—suggests planned deload followed by structured return
HR data reveals positive adaptation: avg HR 158 (06-22) and 163 (06-29) at faster paces (5:50-6:10 min/km) compared to May baseline (6:06-6:14), indicating improved running economy post-recovery
Current week (06-29) shows single 6.8km run at elevated HR (163 bpm)—potential post-training fatigue or ongoing build phase, warrants monitoring before next intensity block
warnings: Large gap between 06-08 and 06-22 (14-day absence from running) suggests injury, illness, or unplanned break—confirm causation to assess readiness
Rising HR at similar/faster paces (163 vs 158) week-on-week could indicate incomplete recovery or overtraining creep; monitor for stagnation
recommendations: Consolidate current momentum: build to 3-4 runs weekly over next 2-3 weeks before adding speed work; you have aerobic base ready
Implement structured HR zones immediately—missing HR data for May-early June masks training intensity; use 06-22/06-29 HR anchors (158-163) to calibrate easy/tempo/threshold runs
Schedule deload week within next 10 days if planning race/peak event; current fatigue ratio (1.18) sustainable but trending toward high with increased volume

weeks_analysis.assessment: Baseline phase: 2 runs, solid 6:06-6:14 paces, 133m elevation. Early-season aerobic building.
Week 2 of block: 1 long run (13.67km), slight volume drop but intensity stable. Unexpected gap post-05-13.
12-day untracked gap. Single 9.5km run, moderate effort. Early deload or recovery week initiated.
Continued taper: 1 run, 7.23km @ 6:12 min/km. Planned reduction suggests upcoming break or event prep.
Minimum volume: 1 run, 6.32km @ 5:47 (fastest May-June). Nadir of deload; complete 14-day absence follows.
Resurgence: 3 runs (football + 2 runs), 15.7km total, avg HR 158. Strong return; soccer session (max HR 188) indicates intensity tolerance recovered.
Post-recovery consolidation: 1 run, 6.85km @ 5:58, HR 163 (elevated but pace strong). Moderate fatigue; structuring next phase advised.
